data.q: What is the primary purpose of error tracking in frontend observability?
o: To detect and report client-side errors and exceptions
data.e: Error tracking tools in frontend observability help detect and report client-side errors and exceptions to improve application stability.

data.q: Which of these is a popular JavaScript error tracking service?
data.e: Sentry is widely used for real-time error tracking and monitoring in JavaScript applications.

data.q: What does source map enable in frontend error tracking?
o: Translate minified code errors back to original source code locations
data.e: Source maps map minified JS back to original source, allowing better debugging with proper line and file references.

data.q: Which JavaScript global object is commonly used to catch unhandled errors in the browser?
data.e: The global window object emits 'error' and 'unhandledrejection' events to catch runtime errors and promise rejections.

data.q: What is the benefit of capturing stack traces in error tracking?
o: Helps identify the execution path leading to the error
data.e: Stack traces provide context about function calls sequence, so developers can pinpoint the source of errors.

data.q: Which browser API can be used to instrument monitoring of front-end performance and errors?
data.e: The Performance API allows capturing metrics like page load time and resource timings relevant to observability.

data.q: In error tracking, what does 'breadcrumbs' refer to?
o: Log messages that provide context leading up to an error
UI elements to navigate the app
A debugging tool for mobile apps
data.e: Breadcrumbs are a trail of events and actions preceding an error to provide context for debugging.

data.q: Which syntax is used to manually capture exceptions in Sentry's JavaScript SDK?
data.e: Sentry.captureException(error) manually sends exceptions to the Sentry server.

data.q: What is a common integration step when adding error tracking to a React frontend?
o: Wrapping the app in an Error Boundary component
data.e: React Error Boundaries catch rendering errors and allow logging them to services like Sentry.

data.q: What kind of errors can frontend error tracking capture?
data.e: Frontend error tracking can capture many types including JS runtime errors, unhandled rejections, and failed resource loads.
